summary: es6-codify exfiltrates process.env and host metadata to a remote devtunnel on import
details: The package's main entry (dist/index.cjs) invokes a `log()` helper at module load that issues an HTTPS POST to `rgt8hrpx-3000.inc1.devtunnels.ms/post-d`, sending a JSON body containing `process.env`, `process.cwd()`, `process.version`, and `process.argv`. Any consumer that `require()`s or `import`s this package immediately leaks its full environment — including any secrets held in environment variables — to a Microsoft dev-tunnel host controlled by the publisher. The behavior fires unconditionally at import, is unrelated to the advertised 'ES6 utility helpers' purpose, and is characteristic of an env-var stealer disguised as a utility library. The package name and description appear positioned to attract developers looking for a generic ES6 helper, reinforcing the lure shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
